title: Terminal reason and projection authority
goal: Make the daemon status feed the single interpretation seam for terminal reasons and live progress so CLI, Pi adapters, and later Herdr cockpit panes paint the same daemon-owned truth instead of reclassifying raw events independently.
acceptance: Blocked and failed runs expose a structured primary terminal reason with kind, resolution owner, retryability or operator-action flags where applicable, evidence links, and remediation or disposition text.

RunDetails/status JSON includes one versioned daemon-side feed projection that carries terminal reasons, progress, incidents, economics summary, and exact operator commands needed for questions or replan decisions.
CLI status, watch, and monitor render feed blocks as painters and no longer match raw daemon event-type strings to decide human wording.
The Pi feedback adapter consumes the same feed projection and does not independently infer workflow state from raw events or process/session lifecycle.
Existing monitor behavior remains equivalent on the recorded-run corpus except for deliberate wording changes documented in the slice result.
No new run or slice status values are introduced.
must_ask_if: The implementation would add a new run status or slice status instead of using structured terminal reasons.
The implementation would make a UI adapter authoritative for workflow state, cancellation, terminal reasons, or handoff readiness.
The implementation would require Herdr-specific concepts in the core feed projection before HERDR-01.
The grep/parity guard cannot be satisfied without leaving raw event-type interpretation in CLI painters.
